---
title: "monte documentation"
---

`monte` is a Python package for quick Monte Carlo modelling, with an emphasis on distribution elicitation and composable models.

This documentation site collects runnable examples and report-style walkthroughs.

## Examples

- [Composing Monte Carlo Models with Distribution Arithmetic](examples/mc-composition.qmd): a new product launch profit model built from elicited distributions and ordinary Python arithmetic.

## Rendering locally

Quarto is distributed as a CLI tool rather than a normal Python package dependency. Install the Quarto CLI from <https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/>, then render the site from the repository root:

```bash
uv run --group docs quarto render docs
```

The generated website will be written to:

```text
docs/_site/
```
